title: Getting an error while trying to build the app
author: Gryk3l
body:
Hi,

First of all, sorry if this is a very dumb/basic question, I am pretty much new in the Android dev. world.

I just cloned the repo, opened it with Android Studio and tried to build it. When I do so, I get the following error:

```
PS C:\<Personal route>\Android-nRF-Blinky> .\gradlew clean build
Starting a Gradle Daemon, 1 incompatible and 4 stopped Daemons could not be reused, use --status for details

> Task :scanner:kaptGenerateStubsDebugKotlin
w: Support for language version 2.0+ in kapt is in Alpha and must be enabled explicitly. Falling back to 1.9.

> Task :app:stripDebugDebugSymbols
Unable to strip the following libraries, packaging them as they are: libandroidx.graphics.path.so.

> Task :scanner:kaptDebugKotlin                                                                                                                                                                          
[WARN] Issue detected with dagger.internal.codegen.ComponentProcessor. Expected 1 originating source file when generating C:\<Personal route>\Android-nRF-Blinky\scanner\build\generated\source\kapt\debug\no\nordicsemi\android\scanner\main\viewmodel\ScannerViewModel_HiltModules_BindsModule_Binds_LazyMapKey.java, but detected 0: [].

> Task :scanner:kaptDebugKotlin FAILED                                                                                                                                                                   

FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:                                                                                                                                                                                       
Execution failed for task ':scanner:kaptDebugKotlin'.
> A failure occurred while executing org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.internal.KaptWithoutKotlincTask$KaptExecutionWorkAction

* Try:
> Run with --stacktrace option to get the stack trace.
> Run with --info or --debug option to get more log output.
> Run with --scan to get full insights.
> Get more help at https://help.gradle.org.

BUILD FAILED in 1m 8s
124 actionable tasks: 124 executed
```

This is probably an error on my side, but I haven't had much luck looking for a solution.

If there is any extra info you want, please feel free to ask for it.

title: beyond true and false
author: ghosalc01
body: is there a way to make the BLE manager work beyond a true/false statement. I've got two switches working between on and off but I need to transfer an uint32 le to a characteristic and I've been unable to do it. I've got the correct uuid characteristic and set up the callback and the data file but I'm stuck.

title: How to parse custom manufacturer data?

Such as power, mac address, version, and so on,

title: Does't not conect device
author: KevinMartinezC
body:
```
Request timed out
Disconnecting...
gatt.disconnect()
cancelOpen() - device: B9:BC:00:23:EB:C7
onSearchComplete() = Device=B9:BC:00:23:EB:C7 Status=0
onClientConnectionState() - status=0 clientIf=15 device=B9:BC:00:23:EB:C7
Services discovered
Device is not supported
Disconnecting...
gatt.disconnect()
cancelOpen() - device: B9:BC:00:23:EB:C7
[Callback] Connection state changed with status: 0 and new state: 0 (DISCONNECTED)
Disconnected
gatt.close()
close()
unregisterApp() - mClientIf=15
```
I was trying to connect a device to use the library but it does not connect

title: Process 'command 'git'' finished with non-zero exit value 128
author: kriskak
body:
Hello,
I downloaded the Repository as .zip, unpacked and tried to import to Android Studio to try it out.
But I get this Error from Gradle sync: Process 'command 'git'' finished with non-zero exit value 128
I do not know what to do. I didn't find the solutions from Stackoverflow and other git issues aplicable for this problem.
What can I do about it?
I am thanking you in advance for your help.

title: How to add different device
author: mkicina
body:
Hi!

I would like to ask if you could provide steps how to connect different device.

title: How to use another device?
author: YouMinJung
body:
Hi.

I want to use other device. (ex. arduino with HM-10)
When I scan the custom device, blinky scans it sucessfully. However, if I connect to this custom device, Blinky returns message that "Device not supported".
I know that Blinky only can connect to nRF5 DK, but I want to use this app with HM-10.

How can I do this? What part of the code should I edit?
please help...

title: How to read a characteristic to a TextView
author: andrewcccc

title: Cannot resolve symbol 'ActivityScannerBinding'
author: kurosh-z
body: Hi , I have an error building the app. It cannot find the ActivityScannerBinding class. I can see inside the ScannerActivity.java that it should be actually imported from: "import no.nordicsemi.android.blinky.databinding.ActivityScannerBinding" but there is no databinding folder inside blinky! Is it an actual bug or do I miss something here?

title: Disconnects from peripherial (nRF for iOS simulator) with not supported error

title: Not connecting with the same device again.
author: nomanhamid
body:
Scanning goes perfect every time

After scanning, if I want to connect with a specific device then it does nothing... Literally nothing, even my device is not null and goes to the connect/Reconnect fucntion.

title: How do you add background services?

body:
The current source goes to the background, and it does not work.

What should I do to add a service so that I can notify you when the application receives a button event in the background?
